old_value: Bryozoa indet crusts has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Rugged bedrock outcrops in the upper infralittoral from 5 to 11.1 m bcd with various sized boulders lying at the base of vertical rock faces with smaller more angular cobbles and shelly stone gravel present in patches. The upper surfaces were covered with red and brown algae beneath a fairly dense canopy of Laminaria hyperborea accompanied by Saccorhiza polyschides (F). The steeper surfaces were dominated by encrusting and bushy bryozoans with dense patches of Corynactis viridis.

Pterothamnion crispum was present in this habitat.

cell_id: 1422.sample_comment,"Rhodophycota has been removed from the species list for this record as more specific related taxa were also present, these are now marked as charactersiing. Upper infralittoral tide-swept bedrock was dominated by Laminaria ochroleuca and Saccorhiza polyschides. old_value: Antithamnion has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value.
